steps.title: Predict the lighting effect
steps.content: The researchers claim that a pattern in time references reflects a behavioral shift caused by electric lighting in the late nineteenth century. If lighting changed behavior, later novels should show more activity at night, especially at times made practical by electric light after its spread.

steps.title: Choose late-night evidence
steps.content: Choice A focuses on 10 a.m. and the broad date 1800. Choice B concerns reference type, not time of day. Choice C gives no historical change. More post-1880 references to activities after 10 p.m. directly supports a lighting-driven behavioral shift toward later activity in daily life. The answer is D.
